name: Dane
context: carpenter; threads: workshop commissions, lake cabin restoration, reunion with an old friend

episodes.text: Milled the walnut boards for the dining table commission and stickered them to dry. Swept the shop, oiled the jointer, and lined up cuts for the weekend.

probes.query: Which wood did I mill for the dining table commission?
I feel workmanlike calm thinking of that prep day in the shop.
Stacked lumber waiting patiently under the window drifts back to me.

episodes.text: Marco messaged out of nowhere after nine years: found my workshop page, said the tool cabinet I built in trade school is still in his garage. We talked until my phone died.

probes.query: What did Marco say is still in his garage?
I feel a rush of happiness remembering Marco reaching out again.
Years of silence ended by a single ping, and the glow of it keeps returning.

episodes.text: First trip of the year to the lake cabin. The roof held through winter but the porch boards went spongy; measured everything and budgeted the lumber on the drive home.

probes.query: What went spongy at the cabin over winter?
I feel practical calm recalling that first cabin inspection.
Walking the old place after the cold months, notebook in hand, comes back to me.

episodes.text: Client rejected the finished bookcase over a stain shade she approved in writing. Held my tongue, photographed everything, and quoted the refinish at full rate.

probes.query: What did the client reject the bookcase over?
I still feel that flare of anger about the bookcase dispute.
Swallowing my first reply and reaching for the camera instead — that moment revisits me.

episodes.text: Restocked screws and glue, replaced two sawblades, and finally labeled the hardware drawers after three years of educated guessing.

probes.query: What did I finally label after three years?
I feel mild satisfaction thinking of that supply-run afternoon.
Order imposed at last on years of rummaging drifts back to me mid-project.

episodes.text: Ripped a board without the guard like an idiot in a hurry and took a kickback bruise across the forearm. Nothing broken, everything humbling. Sat outside for a long while after.

probes.query: Where did the kickback bruise land?
I still feel the scare of that kickback whenever the saw spins up.
The half-second the machine reminded me who's boss replays before every cut.

episodes.text: Delivered the walnut table. The couple's kid immediately claimed the underside as a cave, and they sent a photo an hour later of dinner set for four on it.

probes.query: What did the couple's kid claim the table's underside as?
I feel deep pride remembering the table delivery day.
Something I built becoming the middle of a family's evening stays warm in me.

episodes.text: Marco drove down for the weekend. We rebuilt the cabin porch in two days, argued about joinery like no time had passed, and burned the old boards in a lakeside fire.

probes.query: What did Marco and I burn in the lakeside fire?
I feel full-hearted happiness recalling that porch weekend with Marco.
Two days of hammering beside an old companion, flames by the water after — it returns often.

episodes.text: Quoted three new commissions: a crib, a bench, and a standing desk. Turned down a fourth asking for next-week delivery, politely, then less politely when he called twice.

probes.query: Which three commissions did I quote that day?
I feel lightly irritated recalling that pushy delivery request.
Saying no twice to the same voice on the phone crosses my mind when I schedule.

episodes.text: Dad's old hand plane, seized since he passed, finally freed up after a week in penetrating oil. Flattened a scrap of pine with it and kept the shaving curl on the windowsill.

probes.query: What did I keep on the windowsill after using Dad's plane?
Grief and love mix in me whenever that afternoon with Dad's plane returns.
A tool that remembered his grip waking up in mine — that hour stays lodged deep.

episodes.text: Invoice day: three payments chased, quarterly tax folder sorted, and the van's oil changed. Rewarded myself with dumplings from the corner place.

probes.query: What did I reward myself with after invoice day?
I feel dutiful calm thinking of that paperwork day.
A day of spreadsheets instead of sawdust drifts back when the paperwork season rolls around.

episodes.text: Finished the crib, sanded to four hundred grit, every edge rounded twice. The expecting parents teared up at pickup and I pretended the dust extractor needed adjusting.

probes.query: What grit did I sand the crib to?
I feel moved remembering the parents picking up that crib.
Handing over something made for a person who hasn't arrived yet stays with me.

episodes.text: Cabin weekend: hung the new screen door, planted mint by the steps in Mom's old kettle, and watched a heron work the shallows until dusk.

probes.query: What did I plant mint in by the cabin steps?
I feel deep peace recalling that evening at the lake.
A tall bird fishing in the last light while I sat still — it keeps coming back.

episodes.text: Shop reset for the bench build: sharpened chisels, dressed the grinder wheel, and drew the joinery full-scale on plywood. Cut list taped where the coffee can't reach it.

probes.query: Where did I tape the cut list for the bench build?
I feel ready and calm thinking of that shop reset.
The unhurried ritual before fresh work starts hums in my head on every eve of making.
